mdr_text: IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED A33. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 400 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA TREATED WITH MANUAL INJECTION/INSULIN PEN. CUSTOMER REPORTED THE FOLLOWING LED UP TO THE EVENT: NO TROUBLESHOOTING WAS IDENTIFIED. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-754WWB.  TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED AND THE CUSTOMER REPORTED RECEIVING AN A33 ALARM. CUSTOMER REPORTED HIGH BGS. CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48HRS OF REPORTED HIGH BG EVENT. CUSTOMER DECLINED TO CONTINUE WITH TROUBLESHOOTING. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. MMT-754WWB WAS REQUESTED AND CUSTOMER RESPONSE WAS THE DEVICE WILL BE RETURNED.

INFORMATION RECEIVED BY MEDTRONIC INDICATED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPOGLYCEMIA WITH A BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 1 MMOL/L AND IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE PUMP WAS NOT WORKING PROPERLY. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED AND FOUND THAT THE CUSTOMER HAS TREATED THE LOW BLOOD GLUCOSE EVENT WITH TABLETS AND FRUITS. IT WAS FOUND THAT THE CUSTOMER WAS USING THE PUMP WITHIN 48 HOURS OF THE REPORTED EVENT AND IT WAS UNKNOWN WHETHER THE AUTO-MODE FEATURE WAS ACTIVE. IT WAS FOUND THAT THE DRIVE SUPPORT CAP WAS LOOSE. IT WAS FOUND THAT THE DURING THE LAST SET CHANGE CUSTOMER RECALLS INSULIN DRIPPING OR SQUIRTING FROM END OF SET BEFORE CONNECTING AT THEIR SITE. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. IT WAS UNKNOWN WHETHER THE CUSTOMER CONTINUED TO USE THE INSULIN PUMP. THE INSULIN PUMP WILL NOT BE RETURNED FOR ANALYSIS.

INFORMATION RECEIVED BY MEDTRONIC INDICATED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPERGLYCEMIA WITH A BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 450 MG/DL AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT AND WAS TREATED WITH AN INSULIN PEN FOR THE HIGH BLOOD GLUCOSE. THE CUSTOMER HAD NO SYMPTOMS REPORTED RELATED TO THEIR HIGH BLOOD GLUCOSE. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED AN INSULIN FLOW BLOCK. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED. THE AUTO MODE FEATURE WAS ACTIVE AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT AND THE CUSTOMER WAS USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48 HOURS OF THE REPORTED HIGH BLOOD GLUCOSE EVENT. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. THE CUSTOMER WILL DISCONTINUE THE USE OF THE INSULIN PUMP AND WILL BE RETURNED FOR ANALYSIS.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED NO DELIVERY/OCCLUSION ALARM. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 600 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA WHICH DID NOT REQUIRE TREATMENT. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED CUSTOMER REPORTED THE FOLLOWING LED UP TO THE EVENT: NO TROUBLESHOOTING WAS IDENTIFIED. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1812.  CUSTOMER REPORTED HIGH BGS. CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48HRS OF REPORTED HIGH BG EVENT. CUSTOMER DECLINED TO CONTINUE WITH TROUBLESHOOTING. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-1812.

INFORMATION RECEIVED BY MEDTRONIC INDICATED THAT THE CUSTOMER REPORTED EXPERIENCING HYPERGLYCEMIA AND INSULIN FLOW BLOCKED. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED FOR THE REPORTED EVENT. THE BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS 480 MG/DL AND AT THE TIME OF THE CALL WAS 150 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER WAS TREATED WITH A MANUAL INJECTION. THE CUSTOMER HAD BEEN USING AN INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48 HOURS OF THE REPORTED EVENT AND THE AUTO MODE FEATURE WAS NOT ACTIVE. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED AN INSULIN FLOW BLOCKED ALARM DURING FILL TUBING. THE CUSTOMER CONFIRMED INSULIN DID NOT EXIT AT THE INFUSION SET QUICK RELEASE DURING THE 5U FILL CANNULA. THE CUSTOMER CONFIRMED INSULIN EXITED THE TUBING WITH A MANUAL PUSH OF THE RESERVOIR PLUNGER. THE CUSTOMER ATTEMPTED TO LOAD THE RESERVOIR/FILL TUBING PROCESS AND INSULIN DID NOT EXIT THE TUBING OR PUMP ALARMED. THE CUSTOMER RE-ATTEMPTED THE LOAD RESERVOIR/FILL TUBING PROCESS AFTER A COMPLETE SET CHANGE AND INSULIN EXITED OR THE PUMP ALARMED. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. THE CUSTOMER WILL DISCONTINUE USING THE INSULIN PUMP AND THE PUMP WILL BE RETURNED FOR ANALYSIS.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED POSSIBLE UNDER DELIVERY ANOMALY. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 380 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA TREATED WITH MANUAL INJECTION/INSULIN PEN. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1712K.  TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. MMT-1712K WAS REQUESTED AND CUSTOMER RESPONSE WAS THE DEVICE WILL BE RETURNED.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPERGLYCEMIA BECAUSE THE CUSTOMER REPORTED A POSSIBLE DELIVERY ANOMALY. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED A BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 26 MMOL/L. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA, WHICH WAS TREATED WITH A MANUAL INJECTION/INSULIN PEN AND AN INSULIN PUMP (SUBCUTANEOUS INSULIN INFUSION). SO YOU KNOW, THE TROUBLESHOOTING WAS DONE. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1711K.  THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HIGH BGS. THE CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48 HOURS OF THE REPORTED HIGH BG EVENT AND NO  AUTO MODE/SMARTGUARD FEATURE WAS ACTIVE AT THE TIME OF THE HIGH BG EVENT. CUSTOMER DECLINED TO CONTINUE WITH TROUBLESHOOTING. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. IT WAS UNKNOWN WHETHER THE CUSTOMER WOULD CONTINUE USING THE DEVICE. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-1711K.

INFORMATION RECEIVED BY MEDTRONIC INDICATED THAT THE CUSTOMER CALLED TO REPORT THE INSULIN PUMP WITH A DAMAGED BATTERY CAP. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS NOT PERFORMED AS THE CUSTOMER SENT IMAGES OF THE BATTERY CAP AND THE ISSUE WAS CONFIRMED. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. THE CUSTOMER WILL DISCONTINUE USING THE INSULIN PUMP UNTIL A NEW BATTERY CAP ARRIVES. THE INSULIN PUMP WILL NOT BE RETURNED FOR ANALYSIS.

INFORMATION RECEIVED BY MEDTRONIC INDICATED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPERGLYCEMIA. THE CUSTOMER¿S BLOOD GLUCOSE LEVEL AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS 550 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER TREATED HIGH BLOOD GLUCOSE WITH A MANUAL INJECTION/INSULIN PEN. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED. THE CUSTOMER WAS USING THE PUMP WITHIN 48 HOURS AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT AND IT WAS UNKNOWN WHETHER THE AUTO MODE FEATURE WAS ACTIVE AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT. THE DELIVERY OF INSULIN WAS SUSPENDED DUE TO THE VALUE OF THE SENSOR. THE SYMPTOMS THAT THE CUSTOMER HAD FREQUENT URINATION. THE CUSTOMER HAD AN EMERGENCY KIT AVAILABLE A PEN - LANTUS AND NOVORAPID. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED THAT THE PUMP WAS NOT SENDING TOO MUCH INSULIN. THE CUSTOMER PERFORMED THE HIGH-PRESSURE TEST AND THE TEST WAS PASSED. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. IT WAS UNKNOWN WHETHER THE CUSTOMER WILL CONTINUE USING THE DEVICE AND THE INSULIN PUMP WILL NOT BE RETURNED FOR ANALYSIS.

THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA AND WAS TREATED WITH INSULIN PUMP (SUBCUTANEOUS INSULIN INFUSION). THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1711K. CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48HRS OF REPORTED HIGH BLOOD GLUCOSE EVENT. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. IT WAS UNKNOWN WHETHER THE CUSTOMER WILL CONTINUE THE USE OF THE INSULIN PUMP. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-1711K.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED POSSIBLE UNDER DELIVERY ANOMALY. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 300 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA TREATED WITH DISCONTINUE USE OF INSULIN DELIVERY SYSTEM. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1712KL. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED. CUSTOMER REPORTED HIGH BGS. CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48HRS OF REPORTED HIGH BG EVENT. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-1712KL.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPOGLYCEMIA TREATED WITH GLUCOSE/CARB INTAKE. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED THE FOLLOWING LED UP TO THE EVENT: NO TROUBLESHOOTING WAS IDENTIFIED. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1712KL.  THE CUSTOMER REPORTED LOW BLOOD GLUCOSE. THE CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48 HOURS OF THE REPORTED LOW BLOOD GLUCOSE EVENT. THE CUSTOMER ALLEGES POSSIBLE PUMP OVER DELIVERY AND ALLEGED DELIVERY OF INSULIN WITHOUT USER INPUT. LATER ON THE CUSTOMER DOES NOT BELIEVE THE PUMP WAS OVER-DELIVERING. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. NO PRODUCT RETURN WAS REQUIRED FOR MMT-1712KL.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED LOW BLOOD GLUCOSE. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPOGLYCEMIA TREATED WITH HOSPITALIZATION: OVERNIGHT STAY. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS NOT PERFORMED, AND THE ISSUE WAS NOT RESOLVED. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-523RNAL, UNOMEDICAL, AND MMT-326A. IT WAS UNKNOWN WHETHER THE CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48 HOURS OF THE REPORTED LOW BLOOD GLUCOSE EVENT OR NOT AND WHETHER THE CUSTOMER WAS USED THE AUTO MODE FEATURE OR NOT. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-523RNAL. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR UNOMEDICAL. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-326A.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPERGLYCEMIA TREATED WITH MANUAL INJECTION/INSULIN PEN. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 513 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED NO DELIVERY/OCCLUSION ALARM. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1810. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED. CUSTOMER REPORTED INSULIN FLOW BLOCKED ALARM PASTRESOLVED EVENT. ISSUE WAS RESOLVED. CUSTOMER REPORTED HIGH BGS. CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48HRS OF REPORTED HIGH BG EVENT ALSO USING THE SMARTGUARD/AUTO MODE OF THE INSULIN PUMP. CUSTOMER REPORTED BENT CANNULA NOT A SLIGHT BEND CURVE. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-1810.

INFORMATION RECEIVED BY MEDTRONIC INDICATED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPERGLYCEMIA WITH A BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 578 MG/DL AND WAS TREATED WITH AN INSULIN PUMP. THE CUSTOMER ALSO STATED THAT THEY RECEIVED AN CRITICAL PUMP ERROR/ OPEN BOOK IMAGE ALERT.  NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED AND IT WAS NOTICED THAT THE CUSTOMER WILL DISCONTINUE THE USE OF THE INSULIN PUMP. THE INSULIN PUMP WILL BE RETURNED FOR ANALYSIS.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED MOTOR ERROR ALARM. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 360 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA TREATED WITH MANUAL INJECTION/INSULIN PEN. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-754WWS.  TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED CUSTOMER REPORTED RECEIVING A MOTOR ERROR AS A PAST EVENT. ALARM HISTORY DID NOT SHOW MORE THAN ONE MOTOR ERROR ALARM OR ANY E70 ALARMS WITHIN LAST 30 DAYS. PUMP PASSED DISPLACEMENT TEST. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-754WWS.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED NO DELIVERY/OCCLUSION ALARM. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 266 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA, HEMORRHAGE/BLEEDING TREATED WITH INSULIN PUMP (SUBCUTANEOUS INSULIN INFUSION). THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-754WWS.  TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED AND THE CUSTOMER REPORTED NO DELIVERY ALARM DURING THERAPY AND ALSO REPORTED BENT CANNULA NOT A SLIGHT BEND CURVE AFTER REMOVING SITE PORTION. IT WAS ALSO REPORTED CUSTOMER HAS BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48HRS OF REPORTED HIGH BLOOD GLUCOSE EVENT AND WAS ABLE TO REMOVE INFUSION SET AND IDENTIFIED THE CANNULA WAS BENT AND ALSO REPORTED BLOOD AT SITE. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-754WWS.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPERGLYCEMIA ALONG WITH NO DELIVERY/OCCLUSION ALARM. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 500 MG/DL. THE CUSTOMER REPORTED HYPERGLYCEMIA TREATED WITH MANUAL INJECTION/INSULIN PEN. THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1810. TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED. THE CUSTOMER WAS USING THE INSULIN PUMP WITHIN 48 HOURS AND THE AUTO-MODE/SMARTGUARD FEATURE WAS NOT ACTIVE AT THE TIME OF HIGH BLOOD GLUCOSE EVENT. THE CUSTOMER HAD BEEN USING THE INSULIN PUMP SYSTEM WITHIN 48 HOURS OF THE REPORTED HIGH BLOOD GLUCOSE EVENT. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. PRODUCT RETURN FOR MMT-754WWS IS NOT EXPECTED.

IT WAS REPORTED TO MEDTRONIC MINIMED THAT THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCED HYPERGLYCEMIA WITH BLOOD GLUCOSE VALUE OF 257 MG/DL AND TREATED WITH INSULIN PUMP (SUBCUTANEOUS INSULIN INFUSION). THE EVENT INVOLVED PRODUCT(S) MMT-1810.  TROUBLESHOOTING WAS PERFORMED AND FOUND THAT CUSTOMER REPORTED INSULIN FLOW BLOCKED ALARM PAST EVEN ISSUE WAS RESOLVED. THE CUSTOMER WILL CONTINUE USING THE INSULIN PUMP. NO FURTHER PATIENT COMPLICATIONS WERE REPORTED. NO PRODUCT RETURN IS REQUIRED FOR MMT-1810.
